title: allium howellii subsp. sanbenitense
intro: Allium howellii subsp. sanbenitense, also known as Howell's onion or Howell's garlic, is a plant species within the genus Allium, characterized by its strong onion-like aroma and flavor. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, derived from its sulfur compounds and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Allium howellii subsp. sanbenitense include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and boost the immune system, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, teas, and tinctures, or as a culinary ingredient in various dishes, due to its distinct flavor and aroma.
